zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Warre於1456年出生在英國英格蘭戴文可拉頓 雷利時，她的父親Sir Francis Warre Knight of Collacombe26歲，母親Lady Anne Cuffe21歲。她和John Tremayne至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1504年8月31日在英國英格蘭戴文拉梅頓享年48歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭戴文。

nameMeanings.name: Warre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Warr
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a warrior, usually from Norman French were, werre, waire, warre (central French guerre) ‘war, battle’, and short for de la werre (de la guerre, see Delaware ), literally ‘(man) of the war’, a construction paralleled in de la Bataille (see Battle ). Occasionally it is a shortened form of Old French werreur ‘warrior’.

variants.name: Ware
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by or looked after a dam or weir on a river (from Old English wær, wer, Middle English war(e), warre, wer(e), werre ‘weir’), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Weare (Somerset), Weare Giffard (Devon), or Ware (Hertfordshire).
topographic name for someone who lived by a horse pond or fish pond, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English wayour, which became early modern English ware, wear.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Warre was born in 1456, in Colaton Raleigh, Devon, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Francis Warre Knight of Collacombe, was 26 and her mother, Lady Anne Cuffe, was 21. She had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with John Tremayne. She died on 31 August 1504, in Lamerton, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48, and was buried in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Warre est née en 1456, à Colaton Raleigh, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Francis Warre Knight of Collacombe, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lady Anne Cuffe, avait 21 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec John Tremayne. Elle est décédée le 31 août 1504, à Lamerton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hatte mit John Tremayne mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 31. ja.lifeSummary: Jane Warreが1456年にイギリスイギリスデボンコラトン ラレイグで生まれた時，父Sir Francis Warre Knight of Collacombeは26歳，母Lady Anne Cuffeは21歳であった。彼女はJohn Tremayneとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1504年8月31日にイギリスイギリスデボンラムルトンで48歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスデボンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Warre(이)가 1456년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 콜라턴 라리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Francis Warre Knight of Collacombe(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Anne Cuffe(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 John Tremayne(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1504년 8월 31일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 램르턴에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Warre родилась в 1456 г. в местности колатон реилиг, девон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Francis Warre Knight of Collacombe было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Anne Cuffe, 21. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от John Tremayne. Она умерла 31 августа 1504 г. в местности лэмиртон, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания.
